summary: Listener (registered Democrat, Rockville, Council D7, MD-19, MD-8) is in choose-mode for the June 23 primary, 44 days out. The week's coverage was supposed to be the seven-candidate District 19 House field, but the drain pipeline missed runs on 5/7-5/9; today's run covered Gabriel Sorrel and explicitly flagged the 5/7-5/9 backlog as deferred. Listener's 4/30 daily-note (deep dive on the new MCPS rating system) remains unresolved — the rating system is a Board-of-Ed-not-General-Assembly question, but no Board of Ed candidate has been profiled yet despite the seat being on the ballot. Listener's 4/29 cluster of three questions (fiscal restriction / Andrew Friedson / Wootton) still answered only structurally, not from a Friedson-specific dossier; the Friedson dossier is a deepdive_2026-05-03_friedson brief that was queued and not yet drained.
themes: fiscal discipline vs. spending growth | Listener's standing operating value. None of the seven District 19 House candidates is a fiscal-discipline vote; today's Sorrel arc said this on-air directly. The fiscal-discipline question lives in the County Executive race (Friedson), not in the District 19 House race.

candidate full-history dossiers | The week's intent was to walk Stewart (5/7), Dasgupta (5/8), Sebastian Johnson (5/9), Sorrel (5/10). 5/7-5/9 deferred. Today's Sorrel dossier honestly framed his thinness — no birth year on the public record, no high school, B.S. UMD comp sci with no campus or year, ~20-year OPIS-CoStar-Angi senior software engineer career.

concrete side-by-side policy comparison across the listener's actual races | Today's ep4 ran the seven-candidate District 19 House comparison across ten issues. Three policy axes are uniquely owned by Sorrel — biometric-surveillance bans tied to ICE-cloud-uploads, state domestic-extremism task force, four-day-workweek-for-AI-displacement.

MCPS structural decisions affecting them as a Rockville parent | The 4/30 listener-note ('deep dive of the new rating system') has now been outstanding for 10 days. The rating system is a BoE matter; no BoE candidate has been profiled. Surface a BoE candidate next week or explain on-air the structural-vote-vs-policy-question split for the third time.

sanctuary policy as a litmus test | All seven District 19 House candidates are inside the existing Maryland sanctuary framework. Sorrel is the most aggressive — explicit ban on biometric upload-to-cloud-where-ICE-can-read-it. Listener has been told this answer for three weeks running.
open_questions: What is Andrew Friedson's specific fiscal record and positions? — Owed a focused Friedson dossier; deepdive brief was queued 5/3 but not drained.
What is the new MCPS rating system actually doing — what changes for my kid? — Outstanding since 4/30. Needs a BoE candidate dossier OR a structural-policy explainer.
Why does MCPS want to close and move Wootton High School, and what happens next? — Structural answer given (BoE matter), not closed; Crutchfield arc covered the chronology.
What is Kramer's full historical voting record? — Owed dossier; Kramer is on Crutchfield/Stewart's slate; previously flagged as next priority but skipped during 5/7-5/9 missed runs.

What are the documented policy differences between each candidate on my primary ballot? — Today's ep4 covered District 19 House. County Executive, Council At-Large, BoE, and statewide rows still owed.
Which Democratic primary candidate actually opposes sanctuary policy on illegal immigration? — Answered three times now: NONE in the tier-1 races. Avoid repeating this answer as a framing line.

Gabriel Sorrel: year of birth, place of birth, high school, UMD campus and graduation year, religious self-identification, racial/ethnic self-identification, whether himself LGBTQ-identified or ally member of LGBTQ Democrats of MoCo, 2018 MCDCC platform.
Vaughn Stewart, Sunil Dasgupta, Sebastian Johnson, Alec Stone, Christa Tichy: dossiers and series episodes deferred from 5/7-5/9 missed runs.
Whose Council At-Large endorsement comes when?  — Watch SEIU 500, UFCW 27, MCEA, IAFF 1664 announcements through May into early June.
underserved_topics: Council At-Large field — listener votes for At-Large but no At-Large candidate has been profiled yet; remains tier-1 priority.
MoCo Board of Education race — 4/30 listener question lives here; still unprofiled.
MoCo State's Attorney, Sheriff, Clerk, Register, Orphans' Court — listener has voting rights and zero coverage.
U.S. Representative MD-8 — Raskin race candidates not yet covered.
Andrew Friedson dossier — flagged on 4/29; deepdive brief 5/3 queued, not drained.
Vaughn Stewart, Sunil Dasgupta, Sebastian Johnson, Alec Stone, Christa Tichy — 5/7-5/9 episodes deferred.
Sen. Ben Kramer — not yet given a dedicated arc; listener flagged him by name on 4/27.

db_writes_deferred: weekly_themes UPSERT (window_start=2026-05-04, window_end=2026-05-10) was NOT executed because the project's politics.db schema requires a Windows-side write path (the Linux sandbox can read the table now, but the existing project pattern is for the Windows scheduler to ingest this JSON via main.py). SQL shape matches prior weeks.
